sourceMovie.title: Loving Vincent
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Loving Vincent works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Anomalisa
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Loving Vincent clicked for you, Anomalisa (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: At Eternity's Gate
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Loving Vincent clicked for you, At Eternity's Gate (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Loving Vincent?
faq.answer: Try Anomalisa, At Eternity's Gate, Memoir of a Snail—each shares DNA with Loving Vincent while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Loving Vincent?
faq.answer: Loving Vincent (2017) is best described as Animation, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Loving Vincent worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
